description:
Data contains the list of all airport codes,
the attributes are listed in the table schema. Some of the columns
contain attributes identifying airport locations,
other codes (IATA, local if exist) that are relevant to
identification of an airport.

licenses.name: ODC-PDDL-1.0
licenses.title: Open Data Commons Public Domain Dedication and License v1.0

name: airport-codes

resources.description: List of airports and airfields worldwide with their IATA and ICAO codes, location identifiers, facility type, and geographic coordinates. Derived from the OurAirports public domain dataset by merging latitude and longitude into a single coordinates field.
resources.name: airport-codes

fields.description: OurAirports unique identifier for the airport (e.g. `00A`, `KLAX`, `ZYTX`).
fields.name: ident

fields.description: Full name of the airport or airfield.
fields.name: name

fields.description: Elevation of the airport above sea level in feet. May be empty when unknown.
fields.name: elevation_ft

fields.description: Two-letter continent code (e.g. `NA` = North America, `EU` = Europe, `AS` = Asia, `AF` = Africa, `OC` = Oceania, `SA` = South America, `AN` = Antarctica).
fields.name: continent

fields.description: ISO 3166-2 region code combining country and subdivision (e.g. `US-CA`, `CN-21`).
fields.name: iso_region

fields.description: Name of the city or municipality where the airport is located. May be empty.
fields.name: municipality

fields.description: ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization) 4-letter airport code, used by air traffic control. May be empty for facilities without an ICAO designation.
fields.name: icao_code

fields.description: IATA (International Air Transport Association) 3-letter code, used in passenger ticketing and baggage handling. May be empty for facilities without commercial scheduled service.
fields.name: iata_code

fields.description: GPS navigation code for the airport; often the same as the ICAO code. May be empty.
fields.name: gps_code

fields.description: Local country-specific airport code; primarily used for US airports (FAA identifier). May be empty.
fields.name: local_code

fields.description: Geographic coordinates as a `"latitude, longitude"` decimal string, derived from the source `latitude_deg` and `longitude_deg` columns.
fields.name: coordinates

resources.description: Pre-aggregated count of airports by active facility type. Excludes `closed` airports. Values match the non-closed type values in airport-codes.csv.
resources.name: type-counts

fields.description: Number of airports of this type in the dataset.
fields.name: count

title: Airport Codes

views.description: Distribution of the world's active airports by facility type (excludes closed facilities). Small airports dominate with over 42,000 facilities — the infrastructure of general aviation. Heliports number over 23,000. Large commercial airports handling scheduled passenger services number about 1,178 worldwide, underscoring how concentrated commercial aviation is.
views.name: airports-by-type
views.title: World Airports by Type
